sourceSections: Introduction to Google's Edge AI and Gemma Models
Key Advantages of On-Device AI Deployment
Advanced Capabilities of Gemma 4 Edge Models
Demonstrating On-Device AI with the Gallery App
Lite RT Framework for Cross-Platform Edge Deployment
Performance and Acceleration on Edge Devices

sections.lead: Chintan Parekh introduced Gemma 4 Edge 2B and 4B models, designed for on-device AI, evolving from chatbots to autonomous agents.

bullets.label: New Models
bullets.text: Gemma 4 Edge 2B and 4B are new models introduced for on-device deployment, alongside smaller Gemma 3 models.

bullets.label: On-Device Focus
bullets.text: The session focuses on deploying AI models directly on edge devices efficiently.

bullets.label: Agent Evolution
bullets.text: Gemma 4 represents an evolution from chatbots to more sophisticated autonomous agents with advanced reasoning capabilities.

sections.lead: On-device AI deployment provides key benefits including reduced latency, enhanced data privacy, offline reliability, and cost savings.

bullets.label: Low Latency
bullets.text: On-device deployment offers significantly reduced latency crucial for real-time applications like video filters and calls.

bullets.label: Data Privacy
bullets.text: Enhanced data privacy is achieved by processing sensitive information locally on the device.

bullets.label: Offline Use
bullets.text: Reliable operation is maintained in offline scenarios or environments with poor connectivity.

bullets.label: Cost Reduction
bullets.text: Cost savings are realized by reducing reliance on cloud-based inference, fostering a hybrid cloud-edge approach.

sections.lead: Gemma 4E2B and 4E4B models introduce new agent capabilities including function calling, structured JSON output, and a "chain of thought" feature.

bullets.label: API Integration
bullets.text: The Gemma 4E2B (1-2 GB RAM) and 4E4B models include native support for function calling and tool integration to interact with local and external APIs.

bullets.label: JSON Output
bullets.text: Structured JSON output is built directly into the model architecture of these Gemma 4 Edge models.

bullets.label: Reasoning Mode
bullets.text: A novel "chain of thought" or "thinking mode" is featured for transparent reasoning processes.

bullets.label: Cross-Platform
bullets.text: These models are optimized for hardware to ensure seamless cross-platform deployment.

sections.lead: Google's Gallery app demonstrates Gemma 4 Edge models' agent skills with on-device examples like Wikipedia queries and image understanding.

bullets.label: Wikipedia Query
bullets.text: The Gallery app showcases an agent augmenting knowledge by querying Wikipedia directly on-device.

bullets.label: Personal Tracker
bullets.text: A personal tracker summarizes mood and sleep trends, running entirely on-device within the app.

bullets.label: Image Understanding
bullets.text: An image understanding feature pairs photos with music, all processed locally.

bullets.label: Complex Workflows
bullets.text: The app manages complex workflows, such as identifying animal vocalizations using the on-device model.

bullets.label: Open Source
bullets.text: The Gallery app and its sample code are open-source, encouraging developers to build custom experiences.

sections.lead: Google's Lite RT framework, built on TensorFlow Lite, enables cross-platform AI deployment through model conversion, optimization, and cloud benchmarking.

bullets.label: Core Framework
bullets.text: Lite RT is Google's widely adopted on-device framework, built upon TensorFlow Lite, for AI deployment.

bullets.label: Broad Support
bullets.text: It supports models from various frameworks, including TensorFlow Lite, PyTorch, and JAX, by converting them into the cross-platform TFLite format.

bullets.label: Model Optimization
bullets.text: Tools like Model Explorer help optimize models through techniques such as quantization.

bullets.label: Cloud Benchmarking
bullets.text: The AI Edge Portal provides cloud-based benchmarking to ensure broad Android device compatibility and reliable deployment strategies.

sections.lead: Lite RT delivers robust performance across diverse edge devices, using NPU acceleration for significant speed boosts and energy efficiency.

bullets.label: Wide Compatibility
bullets.text: The Lite RT framework ensures robust performance across Android, iOS, Linux, Raspberry Pi, and other IoT devices.

bullets.label: NPU Boost
bullets.text: NPU acceleration, with integrations for Qualcomm and MediaTek, can yield a 3-10x to 13x performance boost.

bullets.label: Power Efficient
bullets.text: NPU acceleration significantly improves energy efficiency and unlocks real-time AR/VR applications.

bullets.label: Fast Inference
bullets.text: Lite RT demonstrates superior inference speed, being up to 35 times faster than Llama on mobile, with comparable or better performance on other platforms.

bullets.label: Easy Deploy
bullets.text: A CLI tool further simplifies the deployment process for developers.

sections.lead: Lite RT provides a comprehensive, high-performance solution for flexible on-device ML model deployment across various platforms.

bullets.label: Full Solution
bullets.text: Lite RT offers a comprehensive solution for deploying machine learning models on edge devices.

bullets.label: Top Performance
bullets.text: It delivers a high-performance solution with significant performance advantages for on-device AI applications.

bullets.label: Multi-Platform
bullets.text: Lite RT ensures flexibility for deployment across various platforms.

bullets.label: Strong Tooling
bullets.text: Extensive tool support is provided to aid in machine learning model deployment.
